meta.title: A2UI expert harness — reconciled scope: 2 skills + composer/reviewer agent pair · 3 rubrics (.claude/docs/rubrics/) · judge activation (corpus ADR-0060 seam) · procedural compose-verify loop + validate CLI · wiring governance
meta.author: planner (design seat — the expert-harness intake, NEXT item 1)
upstream: .claude/docs/specs/specs/a2ui-training-corpus.spec.md (v0.5 — SPEC-R8 tier-2 bar; R3 AC2 calibration)
.claude/docs/specs/llds/a2ui-corpus-store.lld.md (v0.5 — the realized substrate; §0 deferred tail C8/C12)
.claude/docs/adr/0060-corpus-store-phase1-admission-seams.md (accepted — the judge seam this wave activates)
meta.seat: authoring slices (rubrics/skills/agents): planner-or-host via rubric-author/skill-author/agent-author, graded by the global doc-reviewer/skill-reviewer/agent-reviewer seats; package-code slices (h8/h9): a2ui-builder; activation/eval slices (h10/h13): host-coordinated with a2ui-reviewer as the critic. Gates per slice named in each accept.
meta.deferred: a2ui-jsonl-mcp skill (trigger: the streaming producer wave, NEXT item 2) · programmatic loop driver (trigger: the live-agent wave, NEXT item 4 — streaming LLD-C2's dependency re-points there) · retrieval CLI (trigger: live-agent wave or corpus >20 records) · corpus LLD-C8 contamination + the first eval set + LLD-C12's Inspect-AI scoring/lift leg (trigger: the first eval record; upstream eval/a2ui_eval/{scorers,dataset}.py fetches escalated for THAT wave) · wiring-check promotion to a standing gate (trigger: the first observed wiring-drift incident)
meta.companion: none (v1 — first manifest for this intake)

nodes.label: Expert-harness wave (harness SPEC v0.2 / LLD v0.2)
harness SPEC v0.2 + LLD v0.2 reconciliation (done in this intake)
nodes.accept: a2ui-expert-harness.spec.md at v0.2 and a2ui-harness-wiring.lld.md at v0.2: every v0.1 SPEC-R#/LLD-C# carries a REALIZED-ELSEWHERE (file:line cite) / STILL-NEEDED (reshaped how) / OBSOLETE (why) ruling; rubric home corrected to .claude/docs/rubrics/ (the estate wins over the stale specs/rubrics/ path); SPEC-N2 re-bound to the realized routing_eval.py tripwire + human-read policy; the loop contract (SPEC-R6) split procedural-now / programmatic-at-live-agent; harness_checks.py spec + lld exit 0 on the two docs; all cross-references resolve

nodes.label: ADR-0067 (harness right-sizing) + ADR-0068 (judge verdict-adapter) PROPOSED → ratified
nodes.accept: adr_check.py exits 0 on 0067 and 0068; both carry Status: proposed (never self-ratified) with index rows in adr/README.md; ADR-0068's Repairs names the corpus LLD §0/§1/§11 C12-split edit (applied this change); build slices h3-h9 dispatch only after the host accepts the ADR each encodes

nodes.label: rubric .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-payload.md
nodes.accept: python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-author/scripts/harness_checks.py rubric exits 0 on the file; every dimension typed [gate]/[review] with 1/3/5 anchors and a what-it-checks column; gate-to-promote rule states every [gate] dim hard + gated dims >= 4; [gate] dims CITE the realized deterministic probes (validateA2ui codes via validate-payload, the corpus-data gate) rather than re-judging them (process.md rule 1 — no dimension owns a true/false a script already decides); [review] dims cover composition/idiom (against the seed shelf + catalog docs), binding hygiene, accessibility intent; doc-reviewer (fresh context) scores >= 4 on rubric-author's references/rubric.md gated dims

nodes.label: rubric .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-catalog.md
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py rubric exits 0; [gate] dims cite the REALIZED naming/conformance probes (src/catalog/naming.ts UAX-31 + conformance.ts) as their evidence; [review] dims cover mapping fidelity to ui-* controls, PropDef typing idiom, example/doc coverage per row; gate-to-promote present; anchors cite a realized catalog row (the form-family rows, ADR-0053) as the reference artifact; doc-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: rubric .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-corpus.md (the tier-2 judge standard)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py rubric exits 0; dimensions cover ground-truth validity (CITES a2ui-payload.md for the a2uiOutput leg — no duplicated dimensions), prompt/description quality, target-clarity (the target ?? description consumer rule, ADR-0063), provenance integrity, dedup adjacency; defines the SPEC-R8 aggregation the Judge seam reads: qualityScore = MIN across [gate]-typed dims on the 1-5 scale, passed = qualityScore >= 4; CALIBRATION record committed in the rubric (SPEC-R3 AC2): the same reference record scored in two independent fresh contexts agrees within +/-1 on every gated dim, tolerance stated; doc-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: skill .claude/skills/a2ui-compose/ (patterns + composition, merged R1a+R1b)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py skill exits 0 on SKILL.md (trigger in first ~200 chars; <= 500 lines; validation loop present); description fences siblings (NOT for ui-* control code [component-author], a2ui package source [a2ui-builder], corpus curation [a2ui-corpus-curate], site pages [docs-author]); body encodes the SPEC-R6 bounded loop — compose, run the validate-payload CLI, self-correct, maxRounds 3 then halt-and-report, rubric grading is the CRITIC's not the composer's; references/ carries per-type idiom cards DERIVED from the realized catalog + the 11-seed shelf (file cites, no restated protocol.ts facts); skill-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: skill .claude/skills/a2ui-corpus-curate/ (thin pointer over the realized pipeline)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py skill exits 0; the body is a THIN procedure over the REALIZED pipeline — author seed (src/examples shape, ADR-0055) -> import-seeds --verdicts -> gates (corpus-data.test.ts) -> judge verdicts (a2ui-reviewer against a2ui-corpus.md) -> rescore for back-scoring — citing corpus LLD §6 and ADR-0060..0064 rather than restating them; states the two halt escalations (theta_dup near-dup between distinct seeds; unjudged candidate under a wired judge); NO restated record schema (record.ts owns it); skill-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: agents .claude/agents/a2ui-composer.md + the a2ui-builder.md routing repair
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py agent exits 0 on a2ui-composer.md; frontmatter: scoped tools (Read, Grep, Glob, Write, Bash — Bash justified solely for the validate-payload CLI), model+effort set, maker trigger description with sibling fence; body names 'graded by: a2ui-payload rubric (.claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-payload.md)' and NEVER self-grades (SPEC-R8); charter encodes corpus conditioning (read corpus/exemplar/v1_0/agent-ui.jsonl + the examples pages directly at <= ~20 records; the retrieval CLI is the NAMED scale trigger, not built) and the SPEC-R6 loop with the CLI as the deterministic check; a2ui-builder.md description repaired in the same slice: payload COMPOSITION routes to a2ui-composer, package source/renderer/catalog CODE stays a2ui-builder (both descriptions fence each other); agent-reviewer >= 4 gated on both files

nodes.label: agent .claude/agents/a2ui-reviewer.md (the a2ui critic seat)
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py agent exits 0; critic charter: adversarially grade ONE a2ui artifact (payload | catalog row | corpus record) against the NAMED .claude/docs/rubrics/a2ui-{payload,catalog,corpus}.md rubric, returning severity-classified file:line-cited findings, per-dimension scores, and — when judging corpus records — the ADR-0068 verdicts-file JSON shape; tools Read/Grep/Glob/Bash; no maker verbs in the description (generator ≠ critic structurally); fences the component-reviewer (ui-* controls) and doc-reviewer (prose docs) siblings; agent-reviewer >= 4 gated

nodes.label: tools/harness/validate-payload.ts (the loop's deterministic instrument)
nodes.accept: node --experimental-strip-types packages/agent-ui/a2ui/tools/harness/validate-payload.ts <payload.json> [--catalog agent-ui] exits 0 printing {ok:true, repairs:[...]} on a known-good seed payload (healed form noted when heal changed it) and exits 1 printing structured {code, path, message} failures on planted-invalid fixtures (unknown component · dangling child · malformed pointer · wrong version pin — one fixture each, checked in a slice-report run log); composes heal + validateA2ui + the default catalog via relative imports of the pure core (ADR-0062: the core computes, the shell does IO; the catalog JSON-import Node workaround mirrors import-seeds.ts); zero new deps; npm run check green

nodes.label: judge machinery: src/corpus/judge.ts (pure verdict adapter) + tools/corpus/rescore.ts + import-seeds --verdicts
nodes.accept: npm run check green and npm test green with src/corpus/judge.test.ts: parseVerdictsFile(text) validates the ADR-0068 shape (rubric id + rubricVersion + judgedBy + per-name {qualityScore, passed, failingDimensions?}) rejecting malformed input with structured errors; createVerdictJudge(file) returns a Judge (admit.ts:48 interface) whose score() maps record.name -> verdict; a name ABSENT from the file makes score() throw the named unjudged-candidate error (fail-closed — a wired judge means every candidate must be judged); the corpus barrel (src/corpus/index.ts) exports the adapter (TEN core modules; purity greps stay green); tools/corpus/rescore.ts (Node shell) loads the store, applies verdicts ONLY to records with absent qualityScore, writes meta.qualityScore at/above bar and status:'quarantined' below bar (repair-loop semantics), serializes byte-stably, idempotent on re-run (second run changes nothing); tools/corpus/import-seeds.ts gains --verdicts <path> wiring createVerdictJudge into admit's deps.judge and surfaces the unjudged-candidate throw as report+halt (the theta_dup escalation precedent)

nodes.label: activation: back-score the 11 phase-1 records + prove E_QUALITY end-to-end
nodes.accept: a2ui-reviewer grades all 11 committed shard records against a2ui-corpus.md producing ONE verdicts file (its repo home named in the slice report); rescore run updates corpus/exemplar/v1_0/agent-ui.jsonl: every record carries meta.qualityScore or is status:'quarantined' with the failing dims in the run report; corpus-data.test.ts still green; git diff over the shard shows only qualityScore/status fields changed (byte-stable otherwise); ONE planted below-bar candidate is rejected E_QUALITY through import-seeds --verdicts with its failingDimensions reported (the activation negative control, SPEC-R8 AC2 falsifiable at last — ADR-0060's named trigger honored); the 'absent qualityScore' marker count goes 11 -> 0 (or the quarantine delta is itemized)

nodes.label: routing-trigger corpora + routing_eval tripwire for the two new skills
nodes.accept: each new skill ships scripts/routing-corpus.json (>= 8 positives incl. >= 2 paraphrases, >= 6 negatives drawn from sibling-skill territory: component-author, a2ui-builder dispatch phrasings, docs-author); python3 ~/.claude/skills/skill-author/scripts/routing_eval.py <desc> <corpus> --min-f1 0.7 exits 0 for both; the named misses/grabs are READ and dispositioned in the slice report (the aid is a tripwire, not certification — the SPEC-N2 v0.2 reading); harness_checks description-lint arms stay green

nodes.label: scripts/harness_wiring_check.py (governance + reachability, manual gate)
nodes.accept: python3 scripts/harness_wiring_check.py exits 0: runs harness_checks.py (skill|agent|rubric mode) over the ENUMERATED harness artifact set and asserts reachability — every maker agent's 'graded by' rubric file exists; every skill's referenced tools/gates/rubrics resolve to real paths; every a2ui-*.md rubric is referenced by >= 1 agent or skill (no orphan — SPEC-R5 AC1); a planted dangling rubric reference AND a planted self-grading maker line each make it exit 1 (negative controls, run and shown in the slice report); stdlib-only python; wired as a MANUAL gate (the npm-run-size precedent, ADR-0040 §3) with the promotion trigger recorded in the LLD

nodes.label: reference artifact: the compose→verify loop proven end-to-end (SPEC-N4)
nodes.accept: a2ui-composer, dispatched with a realistic intent and corpus conditioning, produces a payload that (a) passes validate-payload with zero failures and (b) is graded >= 4/5 on every gated a2ui-payload dimension by a2ui-reviewer in a fresh context, within <= 3 compose->verify rounds (the SPEC-R6 bound); the round count, gate outputs, and verdict are recorded in the slice report; the artifact's home is named in the report (candidate seed for a future shelf addition or a committed test fixture — admission NOT required this wave); generator ≠ critic held throughout (composer never saw the rubric scores before finalizing)

actions.label: reconcile the harness docs to the realized corpus-store estate (honest realized/still-needed/obsolete boundary)
pin the right-sizing and judge-mechanism decisions as ratifiable ADRs
define what a good A2UI payload is (the referential standard judgment grades against)
define what a good catalog row is
define what a corpus-admissible record is (the SPEC-R8 tier-2 bar, aggregation included)
teach payload composition as a recurring procedure (patterns + composition + the bounded loop)
teach corpus curation as a thin procedure over the realized pipeline
make payloads corpus-conditioned and mechanically self-checked (the maker seat)
grade a2ui artifacts against the rubrics in a fresh context (the critic seat)
let any seat validate a payload deterministically from the CLI (the loop's floor)
adapt critic verdicts into the admission judge seam (E_QUALITY activation, fail-closed on the unjudged)
back-score the phase-1 unjudged records honestly (score or quarantine, never silent)
trip-wire the skills' routing surface mechanically
keep the harness wiring reachable and governed by a script, not judgment
prove the whole loop on one reference artifact (generation -> gates -> critic, bounded)

edges.why: the rubric's existence/home/gate-role encodes ADR-0067's reshape — authoring before ratification pre-empts the host (corpus-wave n2 precedent)
encodes ADR-0067 (which rubrics exist, where they live)
encodes ADR-0067 (rubric home) AND ADR-0068 (the aggregation the Judge reads)
the skill set (2 not 4, jsonl-mcp deferred) is ADR-0067's ruling
the thin-pointer shape over the realized pipeline is ADR-0067's ruling
the composer/builder routing boundary is ADR-0067's ruling
the critic-seat unit (reviewer agent, not a curator agent) is ADR-0067's ruling
the CLI-as-loop-floor (procedural loop, no programmatic driver) is ADR-0067's ruling
judge.ts/rescore.ts encode ADR-0068's verdict-adapter contract
the composer's frontmatter names a2ui-payload as its grading rubric — the file must exist to reference
the composer's charter preloads/cites the a2ui-compose skill (the procedure it executes)
the reviewer cites the payload rubric it grades with
the reviewer cites the catalog rubric it grades with
the reviewer cites the corpus rubric + emits the verdicts shape its judging role rides
back-scoring grades against the ratified corpus rubric (the standard precedes the verdicts)
the critic seat that authors the 11 verdicts is the h9 agent
rescore/import --verdicts are the machinery the back-score run executes
the routing corpus rides the shipped skill description it evaluates
the routing corpus rides the shipped skill description it evaluates
the wiring check's green run enumerates the shipped rubric files
the wiring check's green run enumerates the shipped rubric files
the wiring check's green run enumerates the shipped rubric files
the wiring check resolves the skill's referenced tools/rubrics
the wiring check resolves the skill's referenced tools/rubrics
the wiring check asserts the maker's graded-by edge resolves
the wiring check asserts the critic cites resolvable rubrics
the reference artifact is composed by the h8 maker seat
the reference artifact is graded by the h9 critic seat
the loop's deterministic check step is the h10 CLI
